prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
French: Le meilleur moment pour planter un arbre, c'était il y a vingt ans. Le deuxième meilleur moment, c'est maintenant.
Note: This translation emphasizes the urgency and importance of taking action, even if it's not the ideal moment, as procrastination only leads to further delays.

German: Die beste Zeit, einen Baum zu pflanzen, war vor zwanzig Jahren. Die zweitbeste Zeit ist jetzt.
Note: This translation captures the essence of the proverb, highlighting that although the perfect time has passed, the present moment is still an excellent opportunity to take action.
